role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified the absence of linguistic cues (blank sign) and stayed within domain.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Analyzed terrain, vegetation, and climate cues consistent with the role.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Identified specific tree species (Loblolly Pine) and vegetation types.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Analyzed road markings, driving side, and infrastructure standards.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized infrastructure and camera rig details to support the conclusion.

visual_consistency_notes.linguistic: Correctly noted the sign is blank/white-backed.
visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Accurately described the mixed pine/deciduous forest and flat terrain.
visual_consistency_notes.botanics: The pine species identified matches the visual morphology of the trees.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Double yellow lines and right-hand driving are clearly visible.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: The utility pole and camera blur are accurately described.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The pipeline correctly identified the United States. The agents provided strong, image-grounded evidence (regulatory markings, specific pine species, utility pole style). The linguistic agent correctly noted the lack of text, while the other four agents provided high-confidence, corroborating evidence for the US Southeast.
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: The judge correctly identified a consensus on North America (specifically the US) from the initial assessments. The reasoning was sound, relying on the strong convergence of regulatory, botanical, and landscape evidence.
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The hypothesis pool (USA, Canada) was perfectly calibrated to the visual evidence. The agents correctly narrowed the candidates to the US based on the specific combination of road markings and vegetation.
